profile.name: Austin Garrod
profile.title: Senior Full Stack Developer
profile.availability: Open to senior full-stack engineering opportunities
profile.tagline: Building maintainable product software across React and Next.js interfaces, Node.js APIs, and data systems.
profile.intro: Senior full-stack developer in Ontario with production experience across React, Next.js, TypeScript, APIs, and databases. Earlier roles include leading web delivery, teaching programming and project management, and building applied research and civic systems. I focus on software that is typed, testable, observable, documented, and straightforward for teams to maintain.
profile.seoDescription: Austin Garrod is a senior full-stack developer in Ontario building maintainable React, Next.js, TypeScript, Node.js, mobile, API, and data systems.

highlights.label: At Epilogue
highlights.description: Day-to-day work across charity-partner product and data systems, including interfaces, APIs, analytics, and AWS reporting.

highlights.label: Product systems
highlights.description: Production software across front-end, API, and data boundaries.

highlights.label: Technical education
highlights.description: Three years teaching programming, web development, project management, and SDLC fundamentals.

highlights.label: Civic systems
highlights.description: Archived public web, API, and mobile work for a community banner program.

skills.title: Frontend engineering
skills.summary: React, Next.js, TypeScript, state management, accessible interfaces, and component systems that stay maintainable as product scope grows.

skills.title: Backend and data
skills.summary: API systems with relational and document databases, background processing, live progress surfaces, rate-limited workers, and service integrations.

skills.title: Native and platform work
skills.summary: Pragmatic native-adjacent systems across Kotlin Multiplatform, Android tooling, C#/.NET, Python, Docker, and local device integrations.

skills.title: Engineering practice
skills.summary: Code review, CI/CD, documentation, mentoring, estimates, SDLC teaching, and AI-assisted workflows grounded in tests and visible reasoning.

experience.summary: Works across charity-partner systems and data workflows spanning React and TypeScript interfaces, Node.js and Express services, MongoDB-backed APIs, analytics, and scheduled AWS reporting.
bullets: Build and maintain charity-partner product surfaces across React and TypeScript frontends, Node.js and Express services, and MongoDB data models, helping make large operational datasets usable for partner teams and internal staff.
Design access boundaries and scheduled AWS reporting and analytics workflows, keeping partner data appropriately scoped while serving charity and leadership reporting needs.
Collaborate directly with company leadership to shape and deliver product and data capabilities in a small team.

experience.summary: Led front-end development, upkeep, and management of corporate web applications across multiple industries.
bullets: Maintained Shopify storefronts for retail clients across front-end and back-end work.
Coordinated external and internal developers to deliver customer and internal software projects.
Reviewed project proposals and estimates while maintaining and extending legacy codebases.

experience.role: Professor, Computer Programming and Project Management
experience.summary: Developed and taught courses in web development, Windows application development, modern JavaScript, and project management.
bullets: Mentored students and alumni on software development choices for early-stage businesses.
Created and maintained online course material focused on practical programming and SDLC fundamentals.
Taught troubleshooting and independent problem-solving as core engineering habits.

experience.employmentType: Freelance; concurrent with teaching from May 2018
experience.summary: Designed and created branded web presences, CMS implementations, and social media presences for organizations.
bullets: Built web experiences with React, Meteor, and established web standards.
Implemented and maintained distributed CMS installations and custom content-management tools.
Helped organizations grow customer and member communication through web and social channels.

experience.summary: Worked on applied research systems including React/Node vehicle analytics, iOS item tracking, and responsive Meteor applications.
bullets: Contributed to a vehicle analytics web application for dealership communication workflows.
Designed and helped develop an iOS application for item-tracking workflows.
Reworked a Meteor web app to improve accessibility across devices for senior users.

projects.title: AustinGarrod.ca
projects.label: Static portfolio and deployment system
projects.summary: A static Astro portfolio built for cPanel hosting, with typed content data, generated resume and social assets, and a PHP contact endpoint.
details: Keeps the public site portable: static pages, no server-side Node dependency, and a contact path that still works on basic shared hosting.
Centralizes profile, navigation, skills, experience, and project copy in typed data so public pages, SEO metadata, and case studies stay consistent.
Generates the Open Graph image and resume PDF from source-controlled scripts, reducing manual asset drift when project or experience copy changes.
Uses a strict design system, GitHub Actions checks, and deployment notes for a low-maintenance professional site that can be rebuilt locally or in CI.
cardHighlights: Static output plus PHP contact handling keeps production compatible with basic shared hosting.
Generated resume and social assets reduce drift between public pages and downloadable material.

caseStudySections.title: Problem
points: The site needed to work on HostPapa/cPanel without a Node runtime while still feeling maintainable as a developer portfolio.
Public content, SEO metadata, resume copy, and social assets needed a single source of truth to avoid stale professional details.

caseStudySections.title: Approach
points: Use Astro for static routes, typed content data for reusable public copy, and a small PHP endpoint for the contact workflow.
Keep the visual system strict and low-maintenance: light surfaces, 1px borders, sharp corners, black actions, and teal accents.

caseStudySections.title: System Shape
points: Astro builds the public pages, sitemap, robots file, structured data, and hashed assets into a static `dist/` directory.
A Python asset script generates the Open Graph image and resume PDF from source-controlled data.

caseStudySections.title: Current State
points: The repository includes local build checks, deployment notes, and GitHub Actions workflows for CI and cPanel upload.
The public GitHub repository is safe to link because it contains the portfolio implementation rather than private client material.

projects.title: Cadence
projects.label: Minimalist podcast player
projects.summary: An independent, account-free podcast player for Android, built with Kotlin Multiplatform around local-first libraries, flexible playback, offline listening, and portable backups.
projects.role: Independent product engineer across Kotlin Multiplatform architecture, playback, imports, diagnostics, Android release validation, and the public product site
details: Built Cadence as an account-free Android podcast player, with subscriptions, playback state, playlists, bookmarks, history, and preferences kept on the device.
Supports public-directory search, direct RSS entry, offline downloads, adjustable playback, chapters and transcripts, OPML, Pocket Casts imports, and Cadence backup and restore.
Keeps crash reporting and sanitized diagnostics transparent and user-disableable, with exact app and website data boundaries documented publicly.
Pairs repeatable Android build, test, device, Android Auto, and release workflows with a public React and TypeScript product site for launch, privacy, and support.
cardHighlights: Account-free, local-first listening covers queues, playlists, bookmarks, history, downloads, and flexible playback.
Public RSS support, portable imports and backups, and user-disableable diagnostics keep listeners in control.

caseStudySections.title: Problem
points: Podcast apps often tie core listening to accounts, cloud services, advertising, or busy discovery surfaces.
The product needed imports, downloads, and playback state to remain reliable through offline use and interrupted operations.

caseStudySections.title: Approach
points: Center the product around local application state and explicit user control instead of accounts or remote synchronization.
Build broad listening features behind a quiet interface, then make diagnostics and data handling visible, documented, and user-disableable.

caseStudySections.title: System Shape
points: Kotlin Multiplatform models subscriptions, episodes, playback state, queues, playlists, bookmarks, history, and import state across the app.
Native Android media integration, device-harness workflows, Android Auto checks, and release builds support repeatable validation.

caseStudySections.title: Current State
points: The public product site, privacy policy, and support documentation are live at cadenceplayer.com, with the Android store release being prepared.
The app is actively versioned and validated across shared code, Android devices, Android Auto, and release builds.

projects.title: Spools
projects.label: Filament inventory with AMS integration
projects.summary: A local web app for tracking 3D printer filament inventory with Bambu Lab AMS data sync over local MQTT.
projects.role: Developer across the React client, Express API, persistence, and local device integration
details: Tracks spool material, color, weight, RFID tags, and manual entries for non-Bambu filament.
Separates machine-synced AMS state from manually curated inventory so automatic updates do not overwrite real-world corrections.
Provides dashboard views for remaining material, loaded trays, spool metadata, and operational checks before long prints.
Ships as a Docker-friendly TypeScript app with SQLite persistence, an Express API, and a React/Vite client designed for local network use.
cardHighlights: Separates machine-synced AMS state from manually corrected inventory data.
Local dashboard surfaces loaded trays, remaining material, and spool metadata before long prints.

caseStudySections.title: Problem
points: Filament inventory needed to reflect both machine-reported AMS data and real-world manual corrections.
The system had to remain useful on a local network without creating a cloud dependency for a workshop workflow.

caseStudySections.title: Approach
points: Model automatic AMS updates separately from manually curated inventory so machine sync does not overwrite corrections.
Expose operational dashboard views for loaded trays, remaining material, spool metadata, and pre-print checks.

caseStudySections.title: System Shape
points: A React/Vite client talks to an Express API backed by SQLite persistence.
Local MQTT integration reads Bambu Lab AMS state while Docker-friendly packaging keeps the app portable.

caseStudySections.title: Current State
points: The private local app is implemented and actively maintained, including AMS synchronization and NFC tag scanning.
Hardware and machine-specific details are kept generic enough for a public portfolio.

projects.title: Charity Data Scraper
projects.label: Resumable Canadian charity data pipeline
projects.summary: A full-stack scraper for public Canadian charity records with queue-based processing, live progress, retries, and MongoDB storage.
projects.role: Developer across the collection pipeline, job controls, persistence, and progress dashboard
details: Uses Redis-backed job state for pause/resume control, retry handling, and rate-limited collection across a large public-record dataset.
Stores raw JSON responses separately from processed MongoDB records so parsing logic can be rerun without re-fetching every source record.
Includes a live progress dashboard, queue visibility, search views, and operational controls for long-running scrape sessions.
Keeps the case study intentionally generic, focusing on pipeline architecture without naming the source site or exposing brittle scraping details.
cardHighlights: Redis-backed job state supports pause/resume control, retries, and rate-limited collection.
Raw JSON and processed records are stored separately so parsing can be rerun without full re-fetches.

caseStudySections.title: Problem
points: Large public-record collection needed to tolerate long runtimes, retries, and interruptions.
Parsing logic needed to evolve without forcing every source record to be fetched again.

caseStudySections.title: Approach
points: Use queue-backed processing with pause/resume controls, retry handling, and rate limiting.
Persist raw source responses separately from processed records so transformation logic can be replayed.

caseStudySections.title: System Shape
points: Node.js and TypeScript coordinate collection, Redis-backed job state, MongoDB storage, and Docker-based local services.
A React dashboard exposes live progress, queue visibility, search views, and controls for long-running sessions.

caseStudySections.title: Current State
points: The private application is implemented with resumable discovery, configurable workers, atomic offset tracking, retries, and source-aware rate controls.
Only public Canadian charity records are in scope; collection settings are designed to respect source limits, and deployments remain responsible for applicable access terms.

projects.title: Kids TV Controller
projects.label: NFC media controller prototype
projects.summary: A private NFC media-controller prototype with an Express API, React configuration dashboard, enclosure design, and unfinished embedded-hardware integration.
projects.role: Developer and prototype designer across the API, dashboard, enclosure, and hardware plan
details: Implements an Express API and React/MUI dashboard around media-library selection, casting control, TV power control, and scheduling.
Keeps NFC card programming and configuration in a separate dashboard while the physical interaction remains intentionally simple.
Models device states such as idle, card detected, casting, blocked, and error feedback across the dashboard and planned hardware.
Includes a reviewed enclosure design and hardware planning for ESP32, PN532 NFC reader, NTAG215 cards, physical controls, and status feedback.
cardHighlights: Separates the NFC selection interface from configuration in a React dashboard.
Working software and enclosure design are documented separately from unfinished embedded-hardware integration.

caseStudySections.title: Problem
points: The prototype needs a simple physical selection interface for an approved media library.
The system bridges card programming, media selection, casting, and planned local hardware feedback.

caseStudySections.title: Approach
points: Use NFC cards as the physical selection interface and keep programming and configuration in a separate dashboard.
Model clear device states such as idle, card detected, casting, blocked, and error feedback.

caseStudySections.title: System Shape
points: The implemented web layer combines an Express API with a React/MUI dashboard, media-library logic, casting control, and scheduling.
The remaining hardware layer covers ESP32 and PN532 integration, physical controls, status feedback, NFC cards, and the reviewed enclosure design.

caseStudySections.title: Current State
points: The API, dashboard, media-control behavior, and enclosure work exist in the private prototype; embedded NFC hardware integration remains unfinished.
Public notes separate implemented software from the remaining hardware responsibilities without exposing household configuration.

projects.title: Honour Our Veterans Banner Platform
projects.label: Civic web, API, and mobile apps
projects.summary: Archived public React, Express, and React Native applications supporting the Uxbridge Honour Our Veterans Banner Program.
projects.role: Developer across the public web, API, and React Native application surfaces
details: Built around public banner map experiences, searchable veteran records, and program administration needs for a community initiative.
Connected web, API, and mobile surfaces so public browsing and internal maintenance could share one underlying project model.
Represents earlier full-stack civic work with React, Express, TypeScript/JavaScript, and React Native across multiple deployable apps.
Kept as an archived public reference to show the engineering shape while avoiding claims that the original program infrastructure is currently maintained.
cardHighlights: Connected web, API, and mobile surfaces around a shared civic project model.
Archived public reference that shows earlier full-stack civic work without implying current maintenance.

caseStudySections.title: Problem
points: A community initiative needed public browsing surfaces and administrative tooling around banner and veteran records.
The implementation had to connect multiple deployable surfaces without fragmenting the underlying project model.

caseStudySections.title: Approach
points: Build public map/search experiences and internal maintenance surfaces around shared API concepts.
Keep the public portfolio framing clear that this is archived civic work, not an actively maintained program infrastructure claim.

caseStudySections.title: System Shape
points: React, Express, TypeScript/JavaScript, and React Native were used across web, API, and mobile applications.
The architecture connected public browsing and internal maintenance to a common project model.

caseStudySections.title: Current State
points: The source reference is public and archived, making it appropriate as a historical engineering example.
The case study avoids suggesting the original civic infrastructure is currently maintained.

projects.title: Campaign Static Site
projects.label: Astro site for municipal campaign content
projects.summary: A production Astro campaign site deployed to Netlify, with static pages, React islands, design system work, and form-driven workflows from a private source repository.
projects.role: Developer across the Astro site, React islands, theme system, and deployment configuration
details: Uses Astro for zero-JS static output by default and React where interactive components are useful.
Deploys through Netlify with a production path aligned around static hosting, deploy previews, and environment-managed form handling.
Organizes page copy, forms, theme definitions, metadata, and reusable content sections for a content-heavy civic site.
Balances campaign-specific messaging with reusable implementation patterns so pages can be revised quickly without reworking the layout system.
cardHighlights: Uses Astro static output by default, with React islands only where interaction is useful.
Organizes content, forms, metadata, and theme definitions for quick campaign revisions.

caseStudySections.title: Problem
points: A content-heavy civic campaign site needed fast revision cycles, static hosting, and form-friendly production behavior.
The implementation needed to balance campaign-specific messaging with reusable page and theme patterns.

caseStudySections.title: Approach
points: Use Astro for zero-JS static output by default and React only for interactive islands that justify it.
Organize copy, forms, theme definitions, metadata, and reusable sections so messaging can change without reworking layout.

caseStudySections.title: System Shape
points: Astro and TypeScript provide the static site foundation, with React and MUI used for interactive or component-heavy surfaces.
Netlify deployment supports static hosting, deploy previews, and environment-managed form concerns.

caseStudySections.title: Current State
points: The site is deployed in production from a private source repository, so the portfolio focuses on implementation patterns rather than campaign operations.
The case study is framed as civic-site architecture without exposing private messaging or operational specifics.
